findings.name: GraphQL mutation without authentication
findings.description: GraphQL mutations detected — ensure authentication is required.
findings.recommendation: Add authentication middleware to all GraphQL mutations.

findings.name: Hardcoded password/secret
findings.description: Password or secret hardcoded in source code.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or secrets manager for credentials.

findings.name: JWT algorithm none or weak
findings.line_content: const token = jwt.sign({ user: "admin" }, JWT_SECRET, { algorithm: "HS256" });
findings.description: JWT using 'none' or weak HS256 algorithm.
findings.recommendation: Use RS256 or ES256 for JWT signing. Never allow 'none' algorithm.

findings.name: JWT algorithm none or weak
findings.description: JWT using 'none' or weak HS256 algorithm.
findings.recommendation: Use RS256 or ES256 for JWT signing. Never allow 'none' algorithm.

findings.name: JWT verify disabled
findings.description: JWT signature or expiry verification is disabled.
findings.recommendation: Always verify JWT signature and expiration claims.

findings.name: Mass assignment — request body to model directly
findings.description: Request body unpacked directly into model — mass assignment vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Use explicit field allowlists. Never pass raw request body to model updates.

findings.name: Admin endpoint without role/permission check
findings.description: Administrative endpoint — ensure proper role-based access control.
findings.recommendation: Add role-based authorisation (e.g. @roles_required('admin'), isAdmin middleware).

findings.name: TLS certificate verification disabled
findings.description: TLS certificate verification disabled — vulnerable to MITM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Always verify TLS certificates in production.

findings.name: NoSQL injection
findings.description: MongoDB/NoSQL query with direct user input — NoSQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitise input. Use schema validation before database queries.

findings.name: Path traversal via user input
findings.description: File path constructed from user input — path traversal vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitise file paths. Use os.path.basename and restrict to allowed directories.

findings.name: Path traversal via user input
findings.description: File path constructed from user input — path traversal vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitise file paths. Use os.path.basename and restrict to allowed directories.

findings.name: Hardcoded API key
findings.description: API key hardcoded in source code.
findings.recommendation: Store API keys in environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.name: Database connection string with credentials
findings.description: Database connection string with embedded credentials.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables for database connection strings.

findings.name: Bearer/OAuth token hardcoded
findings.description: Bearer token hardcoded in source code.
findings.recommendation: Retrieve tokens dynamically from auth server or secrets manager.

findings.name: API endpoint without authentication decorator
findings.description: Flask route accepting write methods — verify authentication is enforced.
findings.recommendation: Add @login_required or authentication middleware to all non-public endpoints.

findings.name: User-controlled URL in server-side request
findings.description: Server makes HTTP request using user-supplied URL — SSRF vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Validate and restrict URLs to an allowlist of domains. Block internal IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x).

findings.name: API endpoint without authentication decorator
findings.description: Flask route accepting write methods — verify authentication is enforced.
findings.recommendation: Add @login_required or authentication middleware to all non-public endpoints.

findings.name: JWT verify disabled
findings.description: JWT signature or expiry verification is disabled.
findings.recommendation: Always verify JWT signature and expiration claims.

findings.name: TLS certificate verification disabled
findings.description: TLS certificate verification disabled — vulnerable to MITM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Always verify TLS certificates in production.

findings.name: CORS credentials with wildcard origin
findings.description: CORS credentials enabled — if combined with wildcard origin, cookies leak to any site.
findings.recommendation: When credentials are enabled, restrict origins to specific trusted domains.

findings.name: SQL injection via string formatting
findings.description: SQL query constructed with string formatting — SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterised queries or ORM methods. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

findings.name: NoSQL injection
findings.description: MongoDB/NoSQL query with direct user input — NoSQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitise input. Use schema validation before database queries.

findings.name: Command injection via user input
findings.description: OS command executed with user-supplied input — command injection.
findings.recommendation: Never pass user input to shell commands. Use parameterised subprocess calls.

findings.name: Path traversal via user input
findings.description: File path constructed from user input — path traversal vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitise file paths. Use os.path.basename and restrict to allowed directories.

findings.name: Express route with ID param lacking auth middleware
findings.description: Express route with ID parameter — ensure authorisation middleware is applied.
findings.recommendation: Add authorisation middleware to verify the caller has access to the resource.

findings.name: Express route with ID param lacking auth middleware
findings.description: Express route with ID parameter — ensure authorisation middleware is applied.
findings.recommendation: Add authorisation middleware to verify the caller has access to the resource.

findings.name: Missing permission check on DELETE endpoint
findings.description: DELETE endpoint — ensure proper authorisation before resource deletion.
findings.recommendation: Add authorisation check verifying the caller has delete permissions.

findings.name: Excessive data exposure — full model serialisation
findings.description: Full model serialised to response — may expose internal/sensitive fields.
findings.recommendation: Use response serialisers with explicit field allowlists.

findings.name: Webhook URL from user input
findings.description: Webhook URL taken from user input — SSRF via webhook registration.
findings.recommendation: Validate webhook URLs and restrict to HTTPS with domain allowlist.

findings.name: Debug mode enabled
findings.description: Debug mode enabled — exposes stack traces, internal paths, and sensitive info.
findings.recommendation: Disable debug mode in production environments.

findings.name: XSS via unsanitised output
findings.description: User input rendered as HTML without sanitisation — XSS vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Sanitise all output. Use context-appropriate encoding.

findings.name: HTTP endpoint (not HTTPS)
findings.description: Non-HTTPS endpoint used — data transmitted in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS for all API endpoints.

findings.name: Internal fields exposed in API response
findings.description: Sensitive or internal field may be included in API response.
findings.recommendation: Exclude sensitive fields from API responses using serialiser allowlists.

findings.name: Sensitive data in logs
findings.description: Sensitive data written to logs.
findings.recommendation: Mask or redact sensitive data before logging.

findings.name: Weak cipher suite
findings.description: Weak cipher suite configured.
findings.recommendation: Use only strong cipher suites (AES-256-GCM, CHACHA20-POLY1305).
